# UAP.WATCH

> Independent visualization layer for the U.S. Department of War's PURSUE program — the 2026-05-08 declassified UAP (Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena, formerly UFO) file release. UAP.WATCH indexes 162 declassified files from war.gov/UFO/ — 120 PDFs, 28 videos, 14 images — covering 26 named incidents from 1947 through December 2025 across FBI, USAF, USN, NASA, State Department, and DoD sources. Built 2026-05-08, indexed 2026-05-09.

This site is the most complete browsable index of the Pentagon's PURSUE Release 01. Every claim is grounded in primary-source government documents. Local PDF text is extracted for 113 of 118 unique PDFs (95.8% coverage). All source URLs link back to war.gov/UFO/ and DVIDS.

## Key facts

- Catalog: PURSUE Release 01 (Presidential Unsealing & Reporting System for UAP Encounters)
- Released: 2026-05-08
- Files: 162 total — 120 PDFs, 28 videos, 14 images
- Indexed incidents: 26 (1 resolved, 8 unresolved, 12 anomalous, 5 corroborated)
- Source agencies: FBI, USAF, USN, NASA, State Department, DoD
- Time range: 1947–2025
- Geographic coverage: Continental US, Atlantic, Mediterranean, Middle East, Indo-Pacific, Central Asia, Lunar surface
- Live URL: https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app
- Source: https://www.war.gov/UFO/

## Topical guides

- [What are UAPs?](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/q/what-are-uaps): Definition and statutory basis (50 U.S.C. § 3373).
- [What is the PURSUE program?](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/q/what-is-pursue-program): Pentagon framework for declassifying UAP records.
- [What is AARO?](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/q/what-is-aaro): All-domain Anomaly Resolution Office.
- [How many UFO files did the Pentagon release in 2026?](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/q/how-many-files-in-pursue): 162 files; full breakdown.
- [GOFAST video explained](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/q/gofast-video-explained): AARO's 2026 resolution of the 2017 Navy clip.
- [The 'Eye of Sauron' UAP case](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/q/eye-of-sauron-orbs): 2023 Western US federal-agent encounter.
- [Has the U.S. government confirmed aliens are real?](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/q/are-aliens-real): Honest summary of the public record.
- [FBI UFO files](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/q/fbi-ufo-files): 1947 Dallas memo through 2023 'Eye of Sauron'.
- [Roswell incident](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/q/roswell-incident): What the FBI memo actually says.
- [Navy UAP encounters](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/q/navy-uap-encounters): GOFAST, GIMBAL, Mediterranean 2024.
- [UFO vs UAP](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/q/ufo-vs-uap): Term history and statutory definitions.
- [Famous UFO videos](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/q/famous-ufo-videos): GIMBAL, GOFAST, FLIR1, PURSUE Greece clip.
- [Are real UFO videos available to the public?](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/q/ufo-videos-real): 28 declassified clips on DVIDS.
- [Famous UFO incidents](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/q/famous-ufo-incidents): The most-cited cases on the U.S. record.
- [UFO sightings by U.S. state](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/q/ufo-by-state): Federal-record geography.
- [Map of declassified UFO sightings](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/q/ufo-map): Interactive Natural Earth map.
- [Apollo lunar UFO anomalies](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/q/lunar-ufo-anomaly): Apollo 12 and Apollo 17.
- [Phoenix Lights](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/q/phoenix-lights): The 1997 Arizona V-formation event.

## Long-form wiki pages

- [UFO Sightings: The 2026 Pentagon Catalog](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/wiki/ufo-sightings): Complete index by era and source.
- [UFO Types and Shapes Reported in Government Files](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/wiki/ufo-types-and-shapes): Orbs, triangles, cylinders, ellipsoids.
- [Evidence for Aliens: What the U.S. Government Has and Hasn't Confirmed](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/wiki/alien-evidence): Honest summary of the record.
- [Pentagon UFO Files: The 2026 PURSUE Release](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/wiki/pentagon-ufo-files): How to access the originals.

## State-by-state UFO records

- [UFO sightings in California](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/state/california): NUFORC volume leader; Battle of LA, Edwards AFB.
- [UFO sightings in Nevada](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/state/nevada): Area 51, Bob Lazar S-4 claims, Nellis range.
- [UFO sightings in Arizona](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/state/arizona): Phoenix Lights (1997).
- [UFO sightings in New Mexico](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/state/new-mexico): Roswell (PURSUE-001), Green Fireballs.
- [UFO sightings in Texas](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/state/texas): Stephenville UFO (2008).
- [UFO sightings in Florida](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/state/florida): Atlantic Navy ATFLIR area, Gulf Breeze.
- [UFO sightings in Washington](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/state/washington): Kenneth Arnold sighting, Maury Island.
- [UFO sightings in Oregon](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/state/oregon): McMinnville UFO photographs.
- [UFO sightings in Idaho](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/state/idaho): 1950 FBI memo (PURSUE-010).
- [UFO sightings in Ohio](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/state/ohio): Wright-Patterson AFB, Project Blue Book HQ.

## Comparison pages

- [GOFAST vs GIMBAL](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/compare/gofast-vs-gimbal): Two famous Navy ATFLIR clips compared.
- [Tic Tac vs GOFAST](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/compare/tic-tac-vs-gofast): Most-cited Navy UAP encounters.
- [Roswell vs Phoenix Lights](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/compare/roswell-vs-phoenix-lights): Documentation vs witness count.
- [AARO vs UAP Task Force](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/compare/aaro-vs-uap-task-force): Pentagon UAP investigation bodies.
- [Project Blue Book vs PURSUE](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/compare/blue-book-vs-pursue): U.S. UFO catalogs across eras.
- [Civilian UFO vs Military UAP](https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app/compare/civilian-ufo-vs-military-uap): Evidentiary structures compared.

## Incidents

Browse all 26 indexed incidents at https://uap-watch-flame.vercel.app — each incident has a dedicated page at /incident/<id> with full narrative, key quotation, source documents, and related videos. Incident IDs follow the pattern PURSUE-001 through PURSUE-026.

## Documents

113 of 118 unique declassified PDFs are indexed with locally-extracted text at /document/<id>. Document IDs follow the pattern DOC-001 through DOC-136. Each page links to the original PDF on war.gov.

## Videos

28 PURSUE-released videos plus 16 supporting videos are indexed at /video/<id>. Each page links to the canonical DVIDS asset.

## Programmatic indexes

- /year/<YYYY> — incidents and documents grouped by year (1947, 1948, 1950, 1969, 1972, 1985, 1994, 1999, 2017, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025).
- /region/<slug> — incidents by geographic region (continental-us, atlantic, mediterranean, middle-east, indo-pacific, central-asia, moon, multi).
- /agency/<slug> — incidents and documents grouped by source agency (fbi, dod, nasa, state-department, us-air-force, us-navy).

## Source data

All UAP.WATCH content derives from publicly-available U.S. government records:
- war.gov/UFO/ — PURSUE Release 01 portal (https://www.war.gov/UFO/)
- DoD press release (https://www.war.gov/News/Releases/Release/Article/4480582/)
- The canonical CSV catalog (war.gov/Portals/1/Interactive/2026/UFO/uap-csv.csv)
- DVIDS — Defense Visual Information Distribution Service (https://www.dvidshub.net/)
- FBI Vault (https://vault.fbi.gov/UFO)

## Citation policy

UAP.WATCH content is citable. Site copyright is independent of U.S. government works (which remain in the public domain per https://www.usa.gov/government-works). Direct quotes from PURSUE source documents should attribute the originating agency and document ID.
